  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "sg-evals/arangodb--a5cca0b8", "path": "arangod/Auth/UserManager.h", "symbol": "UserManager"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/arangodb--a5cca0b8", "path": "arangod/Auth/UserManagerImpl.cpp", "symbol": "UserManager"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/arangodb--a5cca0b8", "path": "arangod/Auth/TokenCache.cpp", "symbol": "TokenCache"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/arangodb--a5cca0b8", "path": "arangod/Auth/TokenCache.h", "symbol": "TokenCache"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/arangodb--a5cca0b8", "path": "arangod/Auth/TokenCache.h", "symbol": "validateJwtHeader"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/arangodb--a5cca0b8", "path": "arangod/Auth/TokenCache.h", "symbol": "validateJwtBody"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/arangodb--a5cca0b8", "path": "arangod/Auth/TokenCache.h", "symbol": "validateJwtHMAC256Signature"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/arangodb--a5cca0b8", "path": "arangod/Auth/TokenCache.h", "symbol": "validateJwtES256Signature"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/arangodb--a5cca0b8", "path": "arangod/Utils/ExecContext.h", "symbol": "ExecContext"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/arangodb--a5cca0b8", "path": "arangod/Utils/ExecContext.h", "symbol": "ExecContextScope"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/arangodb--a5cca0b8", "path": "arangod/Utils/ExecContext.h", "symbol": "ExecContextSuperuserScope"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/arangodb--a5cca0b8", "path": "arangod/GeneralServer/AuthenticationFeature.h", "symbol": "AuthenticationFeature"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/arangodb--a5cca0b8", "path": "arangod/GeneralServer/AuthenticationFeature.cpp", "symbol": "loadJwtSecretKeyfile"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/arangodb--a5cca0b8", "path": "arangod/GeneralServer/AuthenticationFeature.cpp", "symbol": "jwtSecrets"}
  ],
  "text": "Audit findings for ArangoDB authentication and authorization infrastructure:\n\n1. **User Authentication Handler / UserManager** (`arangod/Auth/`):\n   - `arangod/Auth/Handler.h`: Defines the `AuthenticationHandler` interface used for authentication.\n   - `arangod/Auth/UserManager.h` + `arangod/Auth/UserManagerImpl.cpp` / `arangod/Auth/UserManagerImpl.h`: Implement the `UserManager` class which manages user accounts, credentials, and permissions. It queries `_users` system collection and uses `ExecContextSuperuserScope` to bypass permissions during internal user lookups.\n   - `arangod/RestHandler/RestAuthHandler.cpp` / `.h`: Implements the `/_open/auth` REST endpoint for username/password authentication and JWT token issuance.\n\n2. **JWT Token Validation** (`arangod/Auth/TokenCache.cpp` / `arangod/Auth/TokenCache.h`):\n   - The `TokenCache` class implements JWT validation for the REST API. Key methods include:\n     - `validateJwtHeader()` — validates JWT header (algorithm detection, ES256 flag)\n     - `validateJwtBody()` — validates JWT body/payload (expiry, issued-at, etc.)\n     - `validateJwtHMAC256Signature()` — validates HMAC-SHA256 signatures\n     - `validateJwtES256Signature()` — validates ES256 (elliptic curve) signatures\n\n3. **ExecContext / Authorization Context** (`arangod/Utils/ExecContext.h` + `arangod/Utils/ExecContext.cpp`):\n   - The `ExecContext` class (inheriting from `RequestContext`) is the authorization context that checks permissions for database operations. It provides `canUseCollection()` and related methods, and is accessible thread-locally via `ExecContext::CURRENT`. `ExecContextScope` and `ExecContextSuperuserScope` are RAII guards for temporarily changing the context.\n\n4. **`/_api/user` REST Endpoint** (`arangod/RestHandler/RestUsersHandler.cpp` / `.h`):\n   - `RestUsersHandler` implements the `/_api/user` endpoint for user management (create, read, update, delete users and their database/collection permissions).\n\n5. **Default Authentication Settings**:\n   - `arangod/GeneralServer/AuthenticationFeature.cpp` / `.h`: Defines the `AuthenticationFeature` application feature that registers all arangod authentication startup options, including `--server.authentication` (enable/disable auth), JWT secret options (`--server.jwt-secret`, `--server.jwt-secret-keyfile`), and methods like `loadJwtSecretKeyfile()` and `jwtSecrets()` / `hasUserdefinedJwt()`.\n   - `client-tools/Shell/ClientFeature.cpp` / `.h`: Defines arangosh client startup options including `--server.authentication`, `--server.jwt-secret`, `--server.jwt-secret-file`, and `--server.ask-jwt-secret`."
}

User Authentication Handler / UserManager (arangod/Auth/):\n   - arangod/Auth/Handler.h: Defines the AuthenticationHandler interface.\n   - arangod/Auth/UserManager.h + arangod/Auth/UserManagerImpl.cpp / arangod/Auth/UserManagerImpl.h: Implement the UserManager class which manages user accounts, credentials, and permissions. It queries the _users system collection and uses ExecContextSuperuserScope to bypass permissions during internal user lookups.\n   - arangod/RestHandler/RestAuthHandler.cpp / .h: Implements the /_open/auth REST endpoint for username/password authentication and JWT token issuance.\n\n2. JWT Token Validation (arangod/Auth/TokenCache.cpp / arangod/Auth/TokenCache.h):\n   - The TokenCache class implements JWT validation for the REST API. Key methods include:\n     - validateJwtHeader() - validates JWT header (algorithm detection, ES256 flag)\n     - validateJwtBody() - validates JWT body/payload (expiry, issued-at, etc.)\n     - validateJwtHMAC256Signature() - validates HMAC-SHA256 signatures\n     - validateJwtES256Signature() - validates ES256 (elliptic curve) signatures\n\n3. ExecContext / Authorization Context (arangod/Utils/ExecContext.h + arangod/Utils/ExecContext.cpp):\n   - The ExecContext class (inheriting from RequestContext) is the authorization context that checks permissions for database operations. It provides canUseCollection() and related methods, and is accessible thread-locally via ExecContext::CURRENT. ExecContextScope and ExecContextSuperuserScope are RAII guards for temporarily changing the context.\n\n4. /_api/user REST Endpoint (arangod/RestHandler/RestUsersHandler.cpp / .h):\n   - RestUsersHandler implements the /_api/user endpoint for user management (create, read, update, delete users and their database/collection permissions).\n\n5. Default Authentication Settings:\n   - arangod/GeneralServer/AuthenticationFeature.cpp / .h: Defines the AuthenticationFeature application feature that registers all arangod authentication startup options, including --server.authentication, JWT secret options (--server.jwt-secret, --server.jwt-secret-keyfile), and methods like loadJwtSecretKeyfile() and jwtSecrets() / hasUserdefinedJwt().\n   - client-tools/Shell/ClientFeature.cpp / .h: Defines arangosh client startup options including --server.authentication, --server.jwt-secret, --server.jwt-secret-file, and --server.ask-jwt-secret."

# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

**Target Repositories (version-pinned mirrors):**

- `github.com/sg-evals/arangodb--a5cca0b8` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/arangodb--a5cca0b8$` filter

Scope ALL keyword_search/nls_search queries to these repos.
Use the repo name as the `repo` parameter for read_file/go_to_definition/find_references.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

**Sourcegraph Repositories:** `github.com/sg-evals/arangodb--a5cca0b8`

# ArangoDB Authentication and Authorization Audit

## Your Task

Audit the authentication and authorization infrastructure in ArangoDB. Find all C++ source files in `arangodb/arangodb` that implement auth: 1. The file under `arangod/Auth/` or `arangod/RestHandler/` that implements user authentication (look for `AuthenticationHandler` or `UserManager`). 2. The file that implements JWT token validation for the REST API. 3. The file that defines `ExecContext` or the authorization context that checks permissions for database operations. 4. The file under `arangod/RestHandler/` that implements the `/_api/user` REST endpoint for user management. 5. The configuration file or source that defines default authentication settings (`arangosh` or `arangod` startup options). Report each file path and key class/function.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the security domain.

## Available Resources

No local repositories are pre-checked out.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/arangodb--a5cca0b8` (arangodb/arangodb)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
Now I have all the key files. Let me check the `AuthenticationFeature.cpp` briefly for default auth settings and confirm the `RestAuthHandler` content:
Now I have all the information needed. Let me write the answer:

The audit findings have been written to `/workspace/answer.json`. Here's a summary:

## ArangoDB Auth Infrastructure Audit

| # | Topic | File(s) | Key Symbol(s) |
|---|-------|---------|---------------|
| 1 | **User Authentication Handler / UserManager** | `arangod/Auth/Handler.h`, `arangod/Auth/UserManager.h`, `arangod/Auth/UserManagerImpl.cpp` | `AuthenticationHandler`, `UserManager` |
| 2 | **JWT Token Validation** | `arangod/Auth/TokenCache.cpp`, `arangod/Auth/TokenCache.h` | `TokenCache`, `validateJwtHeader`, `validateJwtBody`, `validateJwtHMAC256Signature`, `validateJwtES256Signature` |
| 3 | **ExecContext / Authorization Context** | `arangod/Utils/ExecContext.h`, `arangod/Utils/ExecContext.cpp` | `ExecContext`, `ExecContextScope`, `ExecContextSuperuserScope` |
| 4 | **`/_api/user` REST Endpoint** | `arangod/RestHandler/RestUsersHandler.cpp`, `arangod/RestHandler/RestUsersHandler.h` | `RestUsersHandler` |
| 5 | **Default Auth Settings** | `arangod/GeneralServer/AuthenticationFeature.cpp/.h`, `client-tools/Shell/ClientFeature.cpp/.h` | `AuthenticationFeature`, `loadJwtSecretKeyfile`, `jwtSecrets` |

Also noted: `arangod/RestHandler/RestAuthHandler.cpp` implements the `/_open/auth` endpoint for username/password login and JWT issuance.

  "text": "Audit findings for ArangoDB authentication and authorization infrastructure:\n\n1. **User Authentication Handler / UserManager** (`arangod/Auth/`):\n   - `arangod/Auth/Handler.h`: Defines the `AuthenticationHandler` interface used for authentication.\n   - `arangod/Auth/UserManager.h` + `arangod/Auth/UserManagerImpl.cpp` / `arangod/Auth/UserManagerImpl.h`: Implement the `UserManager` class which manages user accounts, credentials, and permissions. It queries `_users` system collection and uses `ExecContextSuperuserScope` to bypass permissions during internal user lookups.\n   - `arangod/RestHandler/RestAuthHandler.cpp` / `.h`: Implements the `/_open/auth` REST endpoint for username/password authentication and JWT token issuance.\n\n2. **JWT Token Validation** (`arangod/Auth/TokenCache.cpp` / `arangod/Auth/TokenCache.h`):\n   - The `TokenCache` class implements JWT validation for the REST API. Key methods include:\n     - `validateJwtHeader()` — validates JWT header (algorithm detection, ES256 flag)\n     - `validateJwtBody()` — validates JWT body/payload (expiry, issued-at, etc.)\n     - `validateJwtHMAC256Signature()` — validates HMAC-SHA256 signatures\n     - `validateJwtES256Signature()` — validates ES256 (elliptic curve) signatures\n\n3. **ExecContext / Authorization Context** (`arangod/Utils/ExecContext.h` + `arangod/Utils/ExecContext.cpp`):\n   - The `ExecContext` class (inheriting from `RequestContext`) is the authorization context that checks permissions for database operations. It provides `canUseCollection()` and related methods, and is accessible thread-locally via `ExecContext::CURRENT`. `ExecContextScope` and `ExecContextSuperuserScope` are RAII guards for temporarily changing the context.\n\n4. **`/_api/user` REST Endpoint** (`arangod/RestHandler/RestUsersHandler.cpp` / `.h`):\n   - `RestUsersHandler` implements the `/_api/user` endpoint for user management (create, read, update, delete users and their database/collection permissions).\n\n5. **Default Authentication Settings**:\n   - `arangod/GeneralServer/AuthenticationFeature.cpp` / `.h`: Defines the `AuthenticationFeature` application feature that registers all arangod authentication startup options, including `--server.authentication` (enable/disable auth), JWT secret options (`--server.jwt-secret`, `--server.jwt-secret-keyfile`), and methods like `loadJwtSecretKeyfile()` and `jwtSecrets()` / `hasUserdefinedJwt()`.\n   - `client-tools/Shell/ClientFeature.cpp` / `.h`: Defines arangosh client startup options including `--server.authentication`, `--server.jwt-secret`, `--server.jwt-secret-file`, and `--server.ask-jwt-secret`."
}

User Authentication Handler / UserManager (arangod/Auth/):\n   - arangod/Auth/Handler.h: Defines the AuthenticationHandler interface.\n   - arangod/Auth/UserManager.h + arangod/Auth/UserManagerImpl.cpp / arangod/Auth/UserManagerImpl.h: Implement the UserManager class which manages user accounts, credentials, and permissions. It queries the _users system collection and uses ExecContextSuperuserScope to bypass permissions during internal user lookups.\n   - arangod/RestHandler/RestAuthHandler.cpp / .h: Implements the /_open/auth REST endpoint for username/password authentication and JWT token issuance.\n\n2. JWT Token Validation (arangod/Auth/TokenCache.cpp / arangod/Auth/TokenCache.h):\n   - The TokenCache class implements JWT validation for the REST API. Key methods include:\n     - validateJwtHeader() - validates JWT header (algorithm detection, ES256 flag)\n     - validateJwtBody() - validates JWT body/payload (expiry, issued-at, etc.)\n     - validateJwtHMAC256Signature() - validates HMAC-SHA256 signatures\n     - validateJwtES256Signature() - validates ES256 (elliptic curve) signatures\n\n3. ExecContext / Authorization Context (arangod/Utils/ExecContext.h + arangod/Utils/ExecContext.cpp):\n   - The ExecContext class (inheriting from RequestContext) is the authorization context that checks permissions for database operations. It provides canUseCollection() and related methods, and is accessible thread-locally via ExecContext::CURRENT. ExecContextScope and ExecContextSuperuserScope are RAII guards for temporarily changing the context.\n\n4. /_api/user REST Endpoint (arangod/RestHandler/RestUsersHandler.cpp / .h):\n   - RestUsersHandler implements the /_api/user endpoint for user management (create, read, update, delete users and their database/collection permissions).\n\n5. Default Authentication Settings:\n   - arangod/GeneralServer/AuthenticationFeature.cpp / .h: Defines the AuthenticationFeature application feature that registers all arangod authentication startup options, including --server.authentication, JWT secret options (--server.jwt-secret, --server.jwt-secret-keyfile), and methods like loadJwtSecretKeyfile() and jwtSecrets() / hasUserdefinedJwt().\n   - client-tools/Shell/ClientFeature.cpp / .h: Defines arangosh client startup options including --server.authentication, --server.jwt-secret, --server.jwt-secret-file, and --server.ask-jwt-secret."
}
